commit_msg: docs: write codeStory README with full project spec
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Full Confession
subtitle: One minute later, he wrote down everything—the whole blueprint, the whole sickness.
when_where: 09:05 AM, still on main, still in the same breath. The README file is where you tell the world what you're building, but Omkar used it like a suicide note—detailed, methodical, complete. Every spec spelled out. No secrets left.
who_whom: Omkar addressed the void: future collaborators, future selves, future judges. But really, he was talking to himself in the mirror. The project spec became a mirror he couldn't look away from—the more he documented, the more he was documenting his own obsession.
what_why: He wrote it all down: the vision, the scope, the architecture, the purpose. codeStory was supposed to turn git commits into narrative—to make the invisible visible, to give every line of code a voice and a story. But as he documented it, he realized what he was really building: a machine to confess, a system to turn his own work into testimony. Every word in that README was another thread connecting him to what was coming.
verdict: He documented the crime before he committed it.
